content:
[CONTEXT]: I'm investigating the oh-my-opencode (also called oh-my-openagent) project at https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent to understand what named agents like Hephaestus, Prometheus, and others are described in their documentation.

[GOAL]: Find the documentation or README that describes the named agent system (Hephaestus, Prometheus, Sisyphus, etc.) - what they are, how they work, and how they map to the actual agent .md files installed in ~/.claude/agents/.

[DOWNSTREAM]: I need to tell the user whether these conceptual named agents are functional in their local installation, or if they're just organizational concepts/orchestration patterns.

[REQUEST]: 
1. Search the GitHub repo code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent for documentation about Hephaestus, Prometheus, Sisyphus agents
2. Check the docs/ directory for any agent architecture or naming docs
3. Check if there are specific agent .md files or configuration for these named agents
4. Return the relevant documentation excerpts and file paths
<!-- OMO_INTERNAL_INITIATOR -->

todos.content: Clone oh-my-openagent repo and get commit SHA for permalinks
Search repo for Hephaestus/Prometheus/Sisyphus agent documentation
Examine docs/ directory for agent architecture docs
Find agent .md files and configuration for named agents
Synthesize findings with permalinks
